column1: # TIA-102.BABA-A annex_s_interleave.csv — Half-Rate Bit Frame Format
#   3=c3 (14 bits in table); total=72. The c2/c3 split reflects the PDF's own labeling of
#   the bit-stream segments and does not directly map to the u2/u3 codeword boundaries.
# OCR correction applied: symbol 34 bit1 PDF printed "c0(5)" — corrected to "c0(6)".
#   The even-symbol bit1 lane follows c0(23) down to c0(6) across symbols 0
#   c0(5) is covered at symbol 0 bit0; c0(6) would otherwise be missing entirely.
#   This is the same class of PDF typo as the Annex H "c9(5)"→"c0(5)" correction.
# Cross-validated against TIA-102.BBAC-1 Annex E (burst symbol 44 = frame symbol 34):
#   BBAC-1 prints "c0(6) | c1(11)" — matches the post-fix value here. The typo is
#   local to the BABA-A PDF; BBAC-1 (PyMuPDF-extracted) prints this table correctly.
# Extracted from pdftotext -layout output with verification: count=36 (PASS);
#   bit coverage after OCR fix: all 72 bit positions appear exactly once (PASS)
